message:
refactor(security,connector): harden verification to fail-closed and normalize hostname matching

Change lease coordinator verification strategy from threshold-based allow
to strict fail-closed: any invalid signature from a known signer now causes
verification failure, preventing partially-compromised signers from passing.
Introduce SignerVerificationState struct to track valid and invalid
signatures per signer independently.

Normalize hostname matching in network guard to handle trailing dots,
mixed case, and leading/trailing whitespace per RFC hostname conventions.

- connector/lease_coordinator: track per-signer valid+invalid signatures
- connector/lease_coordinator: fail-closed if ANY invalid signature exists
- connector/lease_coordinator: add 5 test cases for duplicates, mixed, unknown signers
- security/network_guard: add normalize_host_for_match() with trim + lowercase
- security/network_guard: add 4 edge case tests (trailing dots, mixed case, whitespace)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
